topic: ChatGPT prompting strategies and real-world use cases

entries.a: Prompts.chat is the world's largest open-source prompt library for AI, designed to work with various AI models including ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama, and Mistral.

entries.q: How can I contribute to prompts.chat?
entries.a: You can contribute by adding prompts at prompts.chat/prompts/new, where they will sync automatically to the library.

entries.q: What is the Interactive Book of Prompting?
entries.a: The Interactive Book of Prompting is a free, interactive guide with over 25 chapters that covers everything from basic to advanced prompting techniques for AI.

entries.q: What age group is the 'Prompting for Kids' program designed for?
entries.a: The 'Prompting for Kids' program is designed for children ages 8-14, using interactive games to teach them how to communicate with AI.

entries.q: What are some advanced prompting techniques mentioned in the content?
entries.a: Advanced prompting techniques include chain-of-thought reasoning and few-shot learning.

entries.q: How can I self-host my own prompt library?
entries.a: You can self-host a prompt library by using the command 'npx prompts.chat new my-prompt-library' followed by 'cd my-prompt-library'.

entries.q: What integrations are available for prompts.chat?
entries.a: Prompts.chat offers integrations such as a CLI, a Claude Code Plugin, and the ability to use it as an MCP server in AI tools.
Prompts.chat has been featured in Forbes, referenced by Harvard and Columbia, and has over 143k GitHub stars.
